reasoning:
The resolution date 2025-11-16 falls before the December 2025 FIDE rating list is released. Therefore, Wikipedia's FIDE rankings page on this date will still reflect the November 2025 official list, where Wesley So is ranked 8th. Since the question asks if his rank will be "as high or higher" (<=8) than his rank on 2025-11-09 (which is also 8th), this will resolve to an exact tie (8 = 8), a clear YES. Thus, I assign a very high probability (95%). 

For 2025-12-09, the December official FIDE list will be reflected on Wikipedia. Wesley So was shockingly eliminated in Round 2 of the FIDE World Cup 2025 in early November by GM Titas Stremavicius (rating 2531). Losing a match with a draw and a loss to a significantly lower-rated opponent in classical chess will cost So approximately 11 Elo points, dropping his rating from 2764 to ~2753. This plunge will likely cause him to fall out of the Top 8 (behind players like Gukesh, Firouzja, Wei Yi, and possibly others). Though upsets at the World Cup might lower the ratings of a few competitors above him, it's highly unlikely enough of them will drop drastically enough to keep So in the Top 8. I allocate 16%.

For 2026-02-07, Wesley So has several months to recover. If he competes in major events like Tata Steel in January 2026, he could regain the lost Elo points and climb back into the Top 8. Given the increased time horizon and natural fluctuations in rankings, my uncertainty increases, pulling the estimate closer to 0.5, so I allocate 38%.
